title: Mildly Spiced Polish Fish Tacos
description: A tangy, crumbly twist on traditional Polish cuisine featuring hard taco shells, dumplings, and a pescatarian-friendly take on a solo dinner.
instructions: Combine the Polish-style fish batter ingredients in a bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Cut the haddock fillets into small pieces and coat with the refrigerated batter.
Heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a large skillet to 375°F (190°C).
Fry the battered fish pieces until golden brown and cooked through.
While the fish is cooking, boil the pierogi dough in a large pot of salted water until they float to the surface.
Drain the pierogi and let them cool slightly.
Warm the taco shells in the oven according to the package instructions.
Fill each taco shell with a fish piece, a few pierogi, and a dollop of Greek yogurt.
Sprinkle the chopped chives and dill pickle relish on top.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
